title: OptimalFactor - Optimal Factor Analysis with EFA-Boosting Algorithm
text: specification_search() runs heuristic hill-climbing search CFA model configurations spirit MacCallum (1986). seed (candidate number factors initial item--factor assignment) algorithm iteratively tries three local operations keeps change lowest composite loss: move item current factor another; drop item low loading causing strain; cov add residual covariance suggested modification indices. bifactor variant (one orthogonal general factor plus configured group factors) can tried automatically every seed k >= 2. procedure returns every model evaluated, subset meets user-supplied fit targets (CFI / RMSEA), best model composite loss. Pass theoretically motivated seeds via seeds argument: complete runnable script available examples/example_specification_search.R. Important caveat — MacCallum (1986). Specification search capitalizes chance. Use exploratory device : Report procedure transparently exploratory. Cross-validate resulting model independent sample via bootstrap. Justify every accepted modification substantive theoretical grounds. function prints warning start every run (suppress verbose = FALSE).
code: library(lavaan) library(OptimalFactor)  # Simulated 3-factor data sim <- '   F1 =~ 0.75*x1 + 0.70*x2 + 0.65*x3   F2 =~ 0.75*x4 + 0.70*x5 + 0.65*x6   F3 =~ 0.75*x7 + 0.70*x8 + 0.65*x9   F1 ~~ 0.30*F2 + 0.30*F3   F2 ~~ 0.30*F3 ' df    <- simulateData(sim, sample.nobs = 500, seed = 2026) items <- paste0("x", 1:9)  res <- specification_search(   data         = df,   items        = items,   max_factors  = 4,   estimator    = "ML",   ordered      = FALSE,   try_bifactor = TRUE,   verbose      = TRUE )  print(res, top = 8)         # ranking by composite loss res$successful              # configurations meeting CFI / RMSEA targets summary(res$best$fit)       # fitted lavaan object for the best model seeds <- list(   "3" = list(     list(F1 = c("x1","x2","x3"),          F2 = c("x4","x5","x6"),          F3 = c("x7","x8","x9"))   ) ) specification_search(data = df, items = items, seeds = seeds,                      estimator = "ML", ordered = FALSE,                      try_bifactor = FALSE)

title: EFA-Boosting Optimization — efa_boosting
text: Performs iterative, machine-learning–inspired optimization Exploratory Factor Analysis (EFA). algorithm integrates: (1) greedy item--item elimination, (2) optional global subset search (multi-item removal), (3) structural rule enforcement (loadings, Heywood / near-Heywood, cross-loadings, minimum items per factor), (4) adaptive minimum interfactor correlation checks, (5) composite fit index whose weights adapt dynamically degrees freedom sample size (df × N), following recommendations Kenny, Shi, Savalei, related literature. function handles ordinal data (WLSMV) includes robust corrections, adaptive weighting rules, optional GPT-based conceptual analysis removed (optionally retained) items. verbose = TRUE, function prints complete EFA-Boosting Optimization Summary Report including: () iteration diagnostics (RMSEA, SRMR, CFI, df, composite loss), (b) identification elimination structural problems, (c) global-search progress bars decisions, (d) thresholded final structure, (e) interfactor-correlation compliance check, (f) AI-based conceptual interpretations. Returns complete list results invisibly.

title: EFA-Boosting Optimization — efa_boosting
text: 1. Optimization strategy. algorithm follows strict hierarchical rule system: Remove Heywood items (ψ < –tol |loading| > 1). Remove near-Heywood items (ψ ≈ 0). Resolve cross-loadings removing items smallest ambiguity gap. Enforce minimum items per factor. structure acceptable RMSEA > target: remove weakest-loading item. use_global = TRUE: evaluate subsets max_drop using composite loss. 2. Adaptive composite loss (df × N). Weights RMSEA / SRMR / CFI adapt dynamically according : degrees freedom (df), sample size (N), estimator (WLSMV boosters), p-close ≥ .05 (bonus). allows stable decisions even RMSEA unreliable (df < 5). 3. Interfactor correlation rule. iteration finalization, factor correlations checked: |φ_ij| < min_interfactor_correlation, warning printed. 4. Global subset search. enabled: evaluates subsets size 1 … k, uses safe-combination caps, shows progress bar, accepts subset removals composite loss strictly improves. 5. AI conceptual analysis. use_ai_analysis = TRUE: removed items receive narrative justification using         loadings, ambiguity gaps, h², ψ, RMSEA--removal, algorithmic reason, retained items can also evaluated, exponential-backoff retry logic ensures robustness, detailed elimination timeline integrated prompt.

title: Exploratory Factor Analysis Optimizer with Optional LLM Support — efa_optimizer
text: Automatically refines Exploratory Factor Analysis (EFA) solution combining global fit (scaled RMSEA), item–level loading quality, explicit detection Heywood/near-Heywood cases. iteration routine re-fits model, removes worst offending item (structural fault RMSEA improvement), stops predefined fit/structure criteria met admissible improvement remains. Optionally, can call Large Language Model (LLM) generate plain-language justifications removing/keeping items, language control detail level.

Additional arguments forwarded PsyMetricTools::EFA_modern().

title: Exploratory Factor Analysis Optimizer with Optional LLM Support — efa_optimizer
text: Iteration logic. Fit EFA using PsyMetricTools::EFA_modern current item set (oblique rotation default). Extract loading matrix \(L\) factor correlation matrix \(\Phi\) (identity unavailable). Derive communalities \(h^2 = \mathrm{diag}(L \Phi L')\) uniquenesses \(\psi = 1 - h^2\). Flag: Heywood (\(\psi < -\)heywood\_tol impossible loadings), near-Heywood (\(\psi\) within [-near\_heywood, near\_heywood]), salient loading (\(|loading| <\) loading), cross-loadings (2+ salient loadings; severity scored gap two largest absolute loadings). Enforce min_items_per_factor using primary (largest absolute) loading; rows salient loading count toward factor. Removal policy (priority order): Remove severe Heywood item. Else remove worst near-Heywood item. Else, RMSEA exceeds thresholds$rmsea, simulate single-item removals (preserve min\_items\_per\_factor) drop candidate yielding best RMSEA improvement. Else remove structurally worst item (diagnostic score). Stop RMSEA \(\le\) threshold factors meet min\_items\_per\_factor, admissible removal improves fit/structure, maximum number steps reached. LLM justifications (optional). use_ai_analysis = TRUE item_definitions provided, function can call LLM via httr draft plain-language justifications. language (ai_config$language) verbosity (ai_config$analysis_detail) control style length. request includes, available, item-level technical statistics (primary loading, \(h^2\), reason removal, RMSEA removal step). Transient server errors (e.g., HTTP 502/503/504) rate limits (HTTP 429) handled three attempts exponential backoff. analysis purely descriptive influence optimization path. Dependencies. what: Confirmatory Factor Analysis Optimization with AI Assistance — optimal_cfa_with_ai
title: Confirmatory Factor Analysis Optimization with AI Assistance — optimal_cfa_with_ai
text: Automatically refines user‐specified Confirmatory Factor Analysis (CFA) model iteratively evaluating fit modification indices. step, function: 1. Fits CFA model computes scaled RMSEA. 2. RMSEA ≤ rmsea_threshold, stops. 3. Otherwise, identifies modification indices (MI) > mi_threshold. 4. Selects largest MI compares standardized loadings two involved items; removes item smaller loading. 5. Records modification, RMSEA MI, updates model syntax dropping removed item, repeats RMSEA acceptable, MI exceed threshold, max_steps reached. Optionally, analyze_removed = TRUE, uses OpenAI API generate concise justifications exclusion retention item, based provided item_definitions factor_definitions.

text: function proceeds phases: Initialization: Parses initial model syntax factor item lists. Filtering: Optionally subsets data via filter_expr. Iterative refinement (max_steps): Fit current CFA model. Compute scaled RMSEA; ≤ rmsea_threshold, stop. Extract MI > mi_threshold; none, stop. Select single largest MI, compare two involved items’ standardized loadings, remove item lower loading. Update model syntax dropping item log step. Final fit: Stores last fitted lavaan object final RMSEA/CFI. Conceptual analysis (optional): removed retained item, calls OpenAI API generate justification text.

Final scaled RMSEA. final_cfi Numeric. Final scaled CFI. conceptual_analysis List sublists removed kept containing AI‐generated texts, NULL performed.

Iterations executed. appear compact two-column table. ASCII evolution plots (RMSEA, SRMR, CFI). show_plot = TRUE, function prints three separate bar-style ASCII diagnostics: RMSEA evolution: bar length increases RMSEA, scaled reference range 0.05–0.13. SRMR evolution: bar length increases SRMR, scaled 0.03–0.10. CFI evolution: bar length increases CFI (higher = better), scaled linearly 0.70 1.00. block includes ASCII axis guide: RMSEA: ticks 0.05, 0.08, 0.10, 0.13. SRMR: ticks 0.03, 0.05, 0.08, 0.10. CFI: ticks 0.70, 0.80, 0.90, 0.95, 1.00. Bars drawn using █ capped configurable length (default: 20 characters). Item removal log. stepwise elimination table mirrors steps_log, formatting available fit indices (RMSEA, SRMR, CFI) three decimals. Final structure. Prints thresholded loading matrix produced efa_boosting (loadings < 0.30 appear 0). Final fit indices qualitative labels. Extracts last row bondades_original prints: Scaled \(\chi^2\), scaled df, RMSEA, CFI, TLI, SRMR. Quick qualitative labels: RMSEA: <= .05 = Excelente, <= .08 = Bueno, <= .10 = Mediocre, else Pobre. CFI/TLI: >= .95 = Excelente, >= .90 = Bueno, else Pobre. SRMR: <= .08 = Bueno, else Pobre. labels intended rapid screening interpreted context. Unicode. report uses UTF-8 box characters (█) accented text. console lacks UTF-8 support, glyphs may degrade.

what: Heuristic Specification Search for CFA Models — specification_search
title: Heuristic Specification Search for CFA Models — specification_search
text: Performs heuristic specification search CFA models spirit MacCallum (1986). seed configuration (fixed number factors), algorithm runs hill-climbing loop evaluates three local operations step: move item factors, drop item, cov (add residual covariance suggested modification indices). Optionally, bifactor variant fitted every seed k >= 2. function returns every configuration evaluated, subset meets user-supplied fit targets, best model composite loss.

title: Heuristic Specification Search for CFA Models — specification_search
code: specification_search(   data,   items,   seeds = NULL,   max_factors = 4,   min_items_factor = 2,   cfi_target = 0.95,   rmsea_target = 0.08,   srmr_target = 0.08,   max_iter_per_config = 40,   max_covs = 5,   max_items_removed = 6,   try_bifactor = TRUE,   operations = c("move", "drop", "cov"),   estimator = "WLSMV",   ordered = TRUE,   std.lv = TRUE,   mi_min = 10,   mi_top = 3,   loss_weights = c(rmsea = 0.5, cfi = 0.3, srmr = 0.2),   patience = 8,   early_stop_after_meet = 3,   verbose = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'specification_search' print(x, top = 10, ...)

title: OptimalFactor 1.1.0
text: run_efa_boosting_wizard(): second Shiny app coexists original run_efa_boosting() studio. Provides guided 5-phase flow (Data → Parallel diagnostic → EFA boosting → Reliability → External convergent/discriminant validity), “proposed action / happen” panel.

title: OptimalFactor 1.1.0
text: Multi-method consensus number factors: Kaiser, parallel analysis (Horn, 1965), MAP (Velicer, 1976) BIC. user can accept consensus recommendation override theoretical value. Trace tab: captures verbose stdout efa_boosting() plus print_conceptual_analysis() output entire item-purification process auditable single pane. Downloadable .txt. AI integration: optional autopilot mode uses OpenAI (gpt-4.1 default) drive wizard step step. Includes chat panel Markdown rendering replies (via commonmark). Reliability: omega (semTools::compRelSEM, ord.scale = TRUE) Cronbach’s alpha per factor, reported alongside CFI/TLI/RMSEA/SRMR EFA-derived CFA model. Automatic detection comparison instruments column names via hierarchical regex (e.g. DERS_AC1, DERS_OB1 → multidimensional instrument two subscales). Mini parallel analysis candidate confirms challenges detected dimensionality. AI auto-classification instrument convergent discriminant based label, using response_format = "json_object". Per-pair verdict using Cohen’s (1988) magnitude conventions: convergencia fuerte / moderada / débil / significativa convergent expectations; discrimina / dudosa / discrimina discriminant expectations. Score construction preserves comparator’s internal structure (sub-totals multidimensional, optional grand total). Heatmap visualization (ggplot2 geom_tile) asterisks p < .05. Downloadable session log (.txt): full audit trail every phase, numerical detail, items removed, fit indices, omega/alpha validity correlations. Downloadable manuscript (.docx): AI-drafted APA-7 sections “2.4 Análisis de datos” “3. Resultados” (subsections 3.1–3.6 convergent instruments present). Tables inserted real session data via placeholders ({{TABLE_LOADINGS}}, {{TABLE_FIT}}, {{TABLE_OMEGA}}, {{TABLE_EXTERNAL}}, {{TABLE_CONVERGENT}}). AI never invents numbers — writes prose around embedded tables. Requires OpenAI API key. Autopilot controls: step exposes ← Back, ⏸ Pause autopilot ▶ Resume AI persistent toolbar. user can rewind previous phase (even “wizard completed” state) without losing previously computed results.
